---
title: "tabviz Studio"
page-layout: full
toc: false
code-tools: false
include-after-body:
  - text: |
      <script>
        // Studio page: make Quarto's chrome go away and let the studio
        // claim the viewport. We intentionally do this client-side rather
        // than via Quarto theming so the rest of the site is unaffected.
        document.documentElement.classList.add("studio-page");
        document.body.classList.add("studio-page");
      </script>
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="studio-assets/studio.css">
          <style>
            /* Studio page: full-bleed; hide Quarto chrome entirely. */
            html, body {
              margin: 0;
              padding: 0;
              height: 100vh;
              overflow: hidden;
              background: #ffffff;
            }
            header.quarto-title-block,
            #title-block-header,
            .quarto-title-block,
            .nav-footer,
            header#quarto-header,
            nav.navbar { display: none !important; }
            main.content,
            div.quarto-content,
            .page-columns,
            #quarto-content,
            #quarto-document-content {
              padding: 0 !important;
              margin: 0 !important;
              max-width: none !important;
              display: block !important;
              grid-template-columns: none !important;
              grid-template-rows: none !important;
              grid-template-areas: none !important;
              height: 100vh !important;
            }
            /* Quarto's Bootstrap CSS pins <aside>, <main>, sidebar columns
               to named grid lines (body-end / page-end / etc.). Those rules
               cascade into the studio shell and push the settings rail to
               the wrong column. Force any descendant of the mount back to
               normal flow positioning. */
            #tabviz-studio-mount,
            #tabviz-studio-mount * {
              grid-column: auto !important;
              grid-row: auto !important;
              grid-area: auto !important;
            }
            #tabviz-studio-mount {
              position: fixed;
              inset: 0;
              background: #ffffff;
              overflow: hidden;
              z-index: 1;
            }
          </style>
---

```{r setup-studio-assets}
#| include: false
#| echo: false
# Stage the studio bundle into the site's resource tree so the page can
# load it via a relative <script>/<link>. Bundle is built separately
# (cd srcjs && npm run build:studio); this just copies it locally.
dest <- "studio-assets"
dir.create(dest, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
src <- system.file("studio", package = "tabviz")
if (!nzchar(src)) src <- file.path("..", "inst", "studio")
for (f in c("studio.js", "studio.css")) {
  ok <- file.copy(file.path(src, f), file.path(dest, f), overwrite = TRUE)
  if (!isTRUE(ok)) warning("studio: failed to stage ", f, " from ", src)
}
```

```{=html}
<div id="tabviz-studio-mount" data-host="docs-static">
  <noscript>
    <p style="padding:24px;font-family:sans-serif;color:#555;">
      tabviz Studio needs JavaScript enabled.
    </p>
  </noscript>
  <p id="tabviz-studio-fallback" style="padding:24px;font-family:sans-serif;color:#888;">
    Loading studio…
  </p>
</div>
<script>
  // Inline probe: confirms that *some* JS runs on this page. If you see
  // this log but not "[tabviz_studio] entry loaded", the bundle failed
  // to parse or fetch.
  console.log("[studio-page] inline probe ran");
  window.__tabvizStudioProbe = true;
  // Catch any error from the bundle load itself.
  window.addEventListener("error", (e) => {
    console.error("[studio-page] window error:", e.message, e.filename, e.lineno);
    const el = document.getElementById("tabviz-studio-fallback");
    if (el) el.textContent = "Studio bundle error: " + e.message;
  });
</script>
<script src="studio-assets/studio.js"></script>
```
